content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 19, 2004

Dennis A. Dickerson
McGuire Environmental Consultants, Inc.
16870 W. Bernardo Center Drive, Suite 440
San Diego, CA 92127

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-04-156

Dear Mr. Dickerson:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the post-governmental employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because we lack specific information identifying your potential client and the particulars of potential communications, we offer informal advice. Informal assistance does not confer the immunity provided by a Commission opinion or formal written advice. (Reg. 18329, subd. (c)(3), copy enclosed.)

QUESTIONS

1. Are you allowed to provide guidance to your client for purposes of compliance with the provisions of a permit it acquired prior to your service on the Los Angeles Regional Water Quality Control Board?

2. May you provide consulting services to assist a client in complying with orders and directives that you issued or voted to issue when you were on the regional board?

3. Are you precluded from advising a client with regard to matters before agencies other than the regional board on which you sat?

4. May you work with other agencies and legislative offices to pursue new options addressing the regulatory concerns of your client?

_________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

File No. 1-04-156
Page No. 2

5. Is your client precluded from conducting educational outreach with various
entities regarding challenges faced in achieving compliance or in seeking new regulatory
approvals to achieve compliance with regional board direction?

6. The client filed an appeal of the cleanup and abatement order which you issued
and that order is in abeyance. You ask whether, assuming the appeal process proceeds to
active status, you could serve as an expert witness on behalf of the client?

CONCLUSIONS

1. Yes. With certain rules limiting your contact with your former employer, you
may advise the client regarding these issues.

2. Since the issuance of the cease and desist orders and notices of violation have
been finalized and issued already, the proceeding in which you had participated is now
complete. Therefore, you may participate in any manner in any issue involving
compliance with the orders, unless prohibited by the one-year ban. So long as advising
your client bears solely to compliance with already issued orders and does not pertain to
the issuance, amendment, awarding or revocation of a permit, or any other prohibited
activities, such representation is not prohibited by the one-year ban.

3. The permanent ban applies only to state administrative agencies. It does not
apply to local agencies. The one-year ban applies only to state administrative agencies.
The one-year ban does not apply to local agencies. However, any communication,
including meetings, with the regional board's staff that is intended to influence any
administrative, legislative or other specified action by the regional board is prohibited by
the one-year ban. Therefore, you are prohibited, for a period of one year, from
participating at a local agency meeting in which regional board staff is present if your
participation is for the purpose of influencing any administrative, legislative or other
specified action by the regional board.

4. The permanent ban applies to judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceedings in
any state administrative agency in which you participated while employed with the
regional board. So long as the governmental bodies with which you wish to work do not
consist of the regional water control board with which you were employed, such activities
are not precluded. The one-year ban applies to the regional board, or any state agency
under that agency's control. As a general rule, you are not restricted by the one-year ban
from lobbying the Legislature or Governor regarding legislation. In addition, you are not
prohibited from influencing administrative actions of agencies that are not under the
control of the regional board.

File No. 1-04-156
Page No. 3

5. The revolving door provisions apply only to former public officials as described in the various provisions. Thus, standing alone and without your advice or participation, nothing prohibits your client from, on its own, pursuing any particular activity. Whether you may advise or consult with a given client depends on the particular parties involved and subject matter. Without more information, we are unable to provide specific advice and refer you generally to the provisions discussed in detail below.

6. See discussion below.

FACTS

You recently left the Los Angeles Regional Water Quality Control Board where you were employed as an executive officer. You have moved to the private sector and are now employed with McGuire Environmental Consultants. You have been contacted by an entity which appeared before the regional board and which wishes to employ your consulting services.

The client is a firm which has received a permit from the regional board. As you understand the Act, you are allowed to provide guidance to the holder of the permit for purposes of compliance with the provisions of the permit and request confirmation of that fact.

The client has received two enforcement actions from the regional board. One is a cease and desist order which was adopted by the regional board. The other is a cleanup and abatement order which you issued. Separately, the client has received several directives from you to provide information and notices of violation. The question which arises is whether you may be able to provide consulting services to assist the client in complying with these issued orders/directives. The nature of compliance is very technical, e.g., ensuring that proper scientific tests are performed, data collection and analysis, report preparation, drafting correspondence for the client to submit to the regional board. In short, ensuring that the client is fulfilling its obligations to comply with the terms of the orders provided. In that these orders have been issued and the client is attempting to comply with their provisions, you would assume that your consulting with the client to achieve compliance would not be precluded.

The client has regulatory relationships with agencies other than the regional board, e.g., Ventura County Air Pollution Control District and the City of Oxnard. The client may need to work with various agencies and legislative offices to secure new approaches to addressing their regulatory concerns. The client may conduct educational outreach with various entities regarding the challenges they face in achieving compliance or in seeking new regulatory approvals to achieve compliance with regional board direction.

File No. I-04-156
Page No. 4

Separately, the client filed an appeal of the cleanup and abatement order which
you issued and that order is in abeyance. In that context, you ask whether, assuming the
appeal process proceeds to active status, you could serve as an expert witness on behalf of
the client?

ANALYSIS

Post-Governmental Employment Restrictions

Public officials who leave state service are subject to two types of post-
governmental employment restrictions under the Act. The first is a permanent prohibition
on advising or representing any person for compensation in any judicial or other
proceeding in which the official participated while in state service. The second is a one-
year prohibition on making any appearance before their former agency for compensation
for the purpose of influencing administrative, legislative or other specified actions.

1. Permanent Ban on "Switching Sides"

Sections 87401 and 87402 prohibit former state administrative officials, who
participated in a quasi-judicial or other proceeding while employed by a state
agency, from being paid to represent or assist in representing another person regarding
that same proceeding. Section 87401 specifically provides:

"No former state administrative official, after the termination of his
or her employment or term of office, shall for compensation act as
agent or attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other person (other
than the State of California) before any court or state administrative
agency or any officer or employee thereof by making any formal or
informal appearance, or by making any oral or written
communication with the intent to influence, in connection with any
judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding if both of the following
apply:

"(a) The State of California is a party or has a direct and substantial
interest.

"(b) The proceeding is one in which the former state administrative
official participated." (§ 87401.)

File No. I-04-156
Page No. 5

Section 87402 provides:

"No former state administrative official, after the termination of his
or her employment or term of office shall for compensation aid,
advise, counsel, consult or assist in representing any other person
(except the State of California) in any proceeding in which the
official would be prohibited from appearing under Section 87401."
(§ 87402.)

As a former state administrative official, you are subject to the permanent ban in
§§ 87401 and 87402 to the extent that you have participated in judicial, quasi-judicial or
other proceedings. "Participated" is defined at § 87400(d) as follows:

"'Participated' means to have taken part personally and substantially
through decision, approval, disapproval, formal written
recommendation, rendering advice on a substantial basis,
investigation or use of confidential information as an officer or
employee, but excluding approval, disapproval or rendering of legal
advisory opinions to departmental or agency staff which do not
involve a specific party or parties." (See also reg. 18741.1.)

Section 87400(c) defines "[j]udicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding" to
include:

"any proceeding, application, request for a ruling or other
determination, contract, claim, controversy, investigation, charge,
accusation, arrest or other particular matter involving a specific party
or parties in any court or state administrative agency, including but
not limited to any proceeding governed by Chapter 5 (commencing
with Section 11500) of Division 3 of Title 2 of the Government
Code."

2. One-Year Ban

In addition to the permanent ban, the Act prohibits specified officials, for one year
after leaving state service, from being paid to communicate with or appear before their
former agency for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative action, or any
action or proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awarding or revocation of a
permit, license, grant, contract or the sale of goods or property. Section 87406(d)(1)
specifically provides that no designated employee of a state administrative agency:

". . . for a period of one year after leaving office or employment,
shall, for compensation, act as agent or attorney for, or otherwise
represent, any other person, by making any formal or informal
appearance, or by making any oral or written communication, before

File No. I-04-156
Page No. 6

any state administrative agency, or officer or employee thereof, for which he or she worked or represented during the 12 months before leaving office or employment, if the appearance or communication is made for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative action, or influencing any action or proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, grant, or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property. For purposes of this paragraph, an appearance before a state administrative agency does not include an appearance in a court of law, before an administrative law judge, or before the Workers' Compensation Appeals Board."

As a former water quality control board member, you are subject to both the permanent ban and the one-year ban.

Question 1. Your first question asks whether you are allowed to provide guidance to your client for purposes of compliance with the provisions of a permit it acquired prior to your service on the board.

Permanent Ban

For purposes of the permanent ban, because the process to determine whether to grant the permit already has been made and because you did not participate in that process, the permanent ban does not prohibit you from advising a client for purposes of complying with the permit.

One-Year Ban

The express language of section 87406(d) restricts your contact with the regional board and any "officer or employee thereof." Thus, you are prohibited from influencing the regional board's civil service staff regarding actions that are administrative or legislative or that involve a permit, license, contract, grant or the sale of goods or property for a period of one year. However, this ban does not prohibit you from advising a client about compliance with the permit, so long as doing so does not involve your contacting your former employer.

2. May you provide consulting services to assist a client in complying with orders and directives that you issued or voted to issue when you were on the regional board?

Permanent Ban

The permanent ban does not apply to a "new" proceeding even in cases where the new proceeding is related to or grows out of a prior proceeding in which the official had participated. A "new" proceeding not subject to the permanent ban typically involves different parties, a different subject matter, or different factual or legal issues from those

File No. I-04-156
Page No. 7

considered in previous proceedings. (See, e.g., Donovan Advice Letter, No. I-03-119.)
We have found generally that proceedings to draft a plan or agreement are different from
proceedings involving implementation of the same plan or agreement, or to amend the
plan or agreement. We have most fully explored this topic in a series of advice letters
treating the often-lengthy administration of environmental cleanup orders. Thus, we have
explained that when the parties to a prior discharge or cleanup proceeding return to an
agency with questions on implementation or amendment of an existing order, a new
proceeding is initiated to which the prohibitions of §§ 87401 and 87402 do not apply.
(Galanter Advice Letter, No. A-82-079; Anderson Advice Letter, No. A-86-324; Pitz
Advice Letter, No. A-88-382; Chalfant Advice Letter, No. A-92-509.) Since the issuance
of the cease and desist orders and notices of violation have been finalized and issued
already, the proceeding in which you had participated is now complete. Therefore, you
may participate in any manner in any issue involving compliance with the orders, unless
prohibited by the one-year ban. (See Bell Advice Letter, No. A-98-295.)

One-Year Ban

Once again, the Act prohibits you from having contact with your former agency
for one year if the appearance or communication is made for the purpose of influencing
administrative or legislative action, or influencing any action or proceeding involving the
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, grant or contract, or
the sale or purchase of goods or property. (§ 87406(d)(1).) So long as advising your
client bears solely to compliance with already issued orders and does not pertain to the
issuance, amendment, awarding or revocation of a permit, or any other prohibited
activities, such representation is not prohibited by the one-year ban.

3. Are you precluded from advising a client with regard to matters before agencies other
than the regional board on which you sat?

Specifically, you ask whether you may advise the client with regard to matters
before the Ventura County Air Pollution Control District and the City of Oxnard.

Permanent Ban

The permanent ban applies only to state administrative agencies. (§ 87400(a).) It
does not apply to local agencies. Thus, the permanent ban does not prohibit you from
communicating with or attempting to influence the agencies identified above.

However, any communication, including meetings, with the regional board's staff
that is intended to influence any judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding of the
regional board is prohibited by the permanent ban. Accordingly, you are prohibited from
influencing regional board staff at a local agency meeting regarding a permit in which you
participated while employed with the regional board.

File No. I-04-156
Page No. 8

One-year Ban

The one-year ban applies only to state administrative agencies. (§ 87400(a).) The
one-year ban does not apply to local agencies. (§ 82041.) Thus, the one-year ban does not
prohibit you from communicating with or attempting to influence a local agency
described above.

However, any communication, including meetings, with the regional board's staff
that is intended to influence any administrative, legislative or other specified action by the
regional board is prohibited by the one-year ban. Therefore, you are prohibited, for a
period of one year, from participating at a local agency meeting in which regional board
staff are present if your participation is for the purpose of influencing any administrative,
legislative or other specified action by the regional board. (See Graves Advice Letter,
No. I-98-141(a).)

4. May you work with other agencies and legislative offices to pursue new options
addressing the regulatory concerns of your client?

Permanent Ban

The permanent ban applies to judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceedings in any
state administrative agency in which you participated while employed with the regional
board. So long as the governmental bodies with which you wish to work do not consist
of the regional water control board with which you were employed, such activities are not
precluded.

One-Year Ban

The one-year ban applies to the regional board, or any state agency under that
agency's control. (§ 87400(d).) As a general rule, you are not restricted by the one-year
ban from lobbying the Legislature or Governor regarding legislation. In addition, you are
not prohibited from influencing administrative actions of agencies that are not under the
control of the regional board. (Monagan Advice Letter, No. A-93-473.) However, you
are prohibited from appearing before other state agency proceedings in which regional
board staff are present, if the appearance is made for the purpose of influencing any
legislative, administrative or other specified action by the regional board. (Grimm Advice
Letter, No. I-96-069.)

5. Is your client precluded from conducting educational outreach with various entities
regarding challenges faced in achieving compliance or in seeking new regulatory
approvals to achieve compliance with regional board direction?

First, the revolving door provisions apply only to former public officials as
described in the various provisions. Thus, standing alone and without your advice or
participation, nothing prohibits your client from, on its own, pursuing any particular

File No. I-04-156
Page No. 9

activity. Whether you may advise or consult with a given client depends on the particular
parties involved and subject matter. Without more information, we are unable to provide
specific advice and refer you generally to the provisions discussed in detail above.

6. The client filed an appeal of the cleanup and abatement order which you issued and
that order is in abeyance. You ask whether, assuming the appeal process proceeds to
active status, you could serve as an expert witness on behalf of the client?

The permanent ban covers any proceeding in which you personally participated
while with the water control board. (Brown Advice Letter, No. A-91-033.) Given that the
order in question and the subject of the appeal is one which you ordered, the permanent
ban generally forbids you from rendering assistance to the client as it seeks to overturn
that order.

However, the permanent ban restricts only compensated activity, it does not
prevent you from representing or assisting any person on a volunteer basis. Further, it
does not prevent you from representing the state or a state agency. (§§ 87401 and 87402.)

Section 87403 also contains some additional narrow exemptions from the
permanent revolving door prohibitions. These generally allow you to provide
uncompensated expert witness testimony or, in specific circumstances, and with the
permission of the Commission or a court, to participate more actively in a proceeding that
was pending during your tenure with the Commission. We encourage you to write for
further advice when the particulars of the potential participation in the appellate process
becomes more concrete.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: C. Scott Pocher
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.analysis:
Post-Govemmental Employrnent Restrictions
Public officials who leave state service are subject to two tlpes ofpost-
govemmental employment restrictions under the Act. The first is a permanent prohibition
on advising or representing any person for compensation in anyjudicial or other
proceeding in which the official participated while in state seryice. The second is a one-
year prohibition on making any appearance before their former agency for compensation
for the pu{pose of influencing administrative, legislative or other specified actions.
1. Permanent Ban on "Switching Sides"
Sections 87401 and87402 prohibit former state adminishative officials, who
participated in a judicial, quasi-judicial or otherproceeding while employed by a state
agency, from being paid to represent or assist in representing anotherperson regarding
that same proceeding. Section 87 401 specifi cally provides :
"No former state administrative official, after the termination of his
or her employment or term of office, shall for compensation act as
agent or attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other person (other
than the State of california) before any court or state administrative
agency or any officer or employee thereofbymaking any formal or
informal appearancg orbymaking any oral orwritten
communication with the intent to influence, in connection with any
judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding if both of the following
apply:
"(a) The State of california is a party or has a direct and substantial
interest.
"O) The proceeding is one in which the former state administrative
official participated." ($ 37401.)
Section 87402provides
"No former state administrative official, after the termination of his
or her employment or term of office shall for compensation aid,
advise, counsel, consult or assist in representing any other person
(except the State of California) in anyproceeding in which the
offi cial would be prohibited from appearing under Section g7 4Ol .-
($ 87402.)
As a former state administrative official, you ulre subject to the permanent ban in
$$ 87401 and 87402 to the extent that you have participated in judicial, quasi-judicial or
other proceedings. "Participated" is defined at $ 87400(d) as follows:
"'Participated' means to have taken part personally and substantially
through decision, approval, disapproval, formal written
recoilrmendation, rendering advice on a zubstantial basis,
investigation or use of confidential information as an officer or
employee, but excluding approval, disapproval or rendering of legal
advisory opinions to deparknental or agency staffwhich do not
involve a specific party or parties." (See also reg. 18741.1.)
Section 87a00@) defines "[i]udicial, quasi-judicial or other proceedingl'to
include:
"any proceeding, application, request for a ruling or other
determination, contract, claim, controversy, investigation, charge,
accusation, arrest or other particular matter involving a specific party
or parties in any court or state administrative agency, including but
not limited to any proceeding governed by Chapter 5 (commencing
with Section 11500) of Division 3 of Title 2 of the Govemment
Code."
2. One-Year Ban
In addition to the permanent ban, the Act prohibits specified officials, for one year
after leaving state service, from being paid to communicate with or appear before their
former agency for the pu{pose of influencing aSministrative or legislative action, or any
action or proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awarding or revocation of a
permit, license, gant, conkact or the sale of goods orproperty. Section S7406(dXl)
specificallyprovides that no designated employee of a state administrative agency:
". . . for a period of one year after leaving office or ernployment,
shall, for compensation, act as agent or attorney for, or other-wise
represent, any other person, by making any formal or informal
appeamnce, or by making any oral or written communication, before


FileNo. I-04-156
any state administrative agency, or officer or ernployee thereof, for
which he or she worked or represented during the 72 months before
leaving office or employment, if the appearance or communication is
made for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative
action, or influencingany action orproceeding involving the
issuance, amendment, awarding or revocation of a permit, license,
grlmt, or contract, or the sale orpurchase of goods or property. For
purposes of this paragraph, an appearance before a state
administrative agency does not include an appearance in a court of
law, before an administrative law judge, orbefore the Workers'
Compensation Appeals Board."
As a former water quality control board member, you are subject to both the
permanent ban and the one-year ban.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Yourfirst question asl<s whether you are allowed to provide guidance to
your clientfor purposes of compliance with the proyisions of a permit it acquired prior to
your service on the board-
Permanent Ban
For purposes of the permanent ban, because the process to determine whether to
grant the permit already has been made and because you did not participate in that
process, the permanent ban does not prohibit you from advising a client for purposes of
complying with the permit.
One-Year Ban
The express language of section 87406(d) restricts your contact with the regional
board and any "officer or employee thereof." Thus, you are prohibited from influencing
the regional board's civil service staff regarding actions that are administrative or
legislative or that involve a permit, license, contract, grant or the sale of goods or property
for a period of one year. However, this ban does not prohibit you from advising a client
about compliance with the permit, so long as doing so does not involve your contacting
your former employer.
2. May you provide consulting services to assist a client in complying with orders and
directives that you issued or voted to issue when you were on the regional board?
Permanent Ban
The permanent ban does not apply to a "new" proceeding even in cases where the
new proceeding is related to or grows out of a prior proceeding in which the official had
participated. A "new" proceeding not subject to the permanent ban typically involves
different parties, a different subject matter, or different factual or legal issues from those
considered in previous proceedings. (See, e.g., Donovan Advice Letter, No. I-03-1 19.)
We have found generally that proceedings to draft a plan or agreement are different from
proceedings involving implementation of the same plan or agreement, or to amend the
plan or agreement. 'We have most fully explored this topic in a series of advice letters
treating the often-lengthy administration of environmental cleanup orders. Thus, we have
explained that when the parties to a prior discharge or cleanup proceeding retum to an
agency with questions on implementation or amendment of an existing order, a new
proceeding is initiated to which the prohibitions of $$ 87401 and87402 do not apply.
(Galanter Advice Letter, No. A-82-079; Anderson Advice Letter, No. 4-86-324; Witz
Advice Letter, No. 4-88-382; Chalfant Advice Letter, No. A-92-509.) Since the issuance
of the cease and desist orders and notices of yiolation have been finalized and,issued
already, the proceeding in which you had participated is now complete. Therefore, you
may participate in any manner in any issue involving compliance with the orders, unless
prohibited by the one-yearban. (See Bell Adice Letter, No. A-98-295.)
One-Year Ban
Once again, the Act prohibits you from having contact with your former agency
for one year if the appearance or communication is made for the purpose of influencing
administrative or legislative action, or influencing any action orproceeding involving the
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permi! licensg Smt, or contract, or
the sale or purchase of goods or property. ($ 87406(dxl).) So long as advising your
client bears solely to compliance with already issued orders and does not pertain to the
issuance, amendment, awarding or revocation of a permit, or any other prohibited
activities, such representation is not prohibited by the one-year ban.
3. Are you precludedfrom advising a client wtth regard to matters before agencies other
than the regional board on which you sat?
Specifically, you ask whether you may advise the client with regard to maffers
before the Ventura County Air Pollution Control District and the City of Oxnard.
Permanent Ban
The permanent ban applies only to state administrative agencies. (g S7a00(a).) It
does not apply to local agencies. Thus, the permanent ban does not prohibit you from
communicating with or attempting to influence the agencies identified above.
However, any communication, including meetings, with the regional board's staff
that is intended to influence anyjudicial, quasi-judicial or otherproceeding of the
regional board is prohibited by the permanent ban. Accordirgly, you are prohibited from
influencing regional board staffat a local agency meeting regmding a permit in which you
participated while employed with the regional board.


File No. l-04-156
One-year Ban
The one-year ban applies only to state administrative agencies. (g S7a00(a).) The
one-year ban does not apply to local agencies. ($ 82041.) Thus, the one-year ban does not
prohibit you from communicating with or attempting to influe,nce a local agency
described above.
However, any coilrmunication, including meetings, with the regional board's staff
that is intended to influence any administrative, legislative or other specified action by the
regional board is prohibited by the one-year ban. Thereforg you are prohibited, for a
period of one year, from participatin g at a local agency meeting in which regional board
staffare present if your participation is for the purpose of influencing any administrative,
legislative or other specified action by the regional board. (See Graves Advice Leffer,
No. I-98-141(a).)
4. May you workwith other agencies and legislative ffices to pursue new opttons
addressing the regulatory concerns ofyour client?
Permanent Ban
The permanent ban applies to judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceedings in any
state administrative agency in which you participated while employed with the regional
board. So long as the governmental bodies with which you wish to work do not consist
of the regional water conkol board with which you were employed, such activities are not
precluded.
One-Year Ban
The one-year ban applies to the regional board, or any state agency under that
agency's conkol. ($ 87406(d).) As a general rule, you me not restricted by the one-year
ban from lobblng the Legislature or Governor regarding legislation. In addition, you are
not prohibited from influencing administrative actions of agencies that are not under the
control of the regional board. (Monagan Advice Letter, No. A-93-473.) However, you
are prohibited from appearing before other state agencyproceedings in which regional
board staffare present, if the appearance is made for the purpose of influencing any
legislative, administrative or other specified action bytheregional board. (GimmAdvice
Letter, No. I-96-069.)
5. Is your client precludedfrom conducting educational outreach with vaious entities
regarding challengesfaced in achieving compliance or in seeking new regulatory
approvals to achieve compliance with regional board direction?
First, the revolving door provisions apply only to former public officials as
described in the various provisions. Thus, standing alone and without your advice or
participation, nothing prohibits your client from, on its own, pursuing any particular
activity. Whether you may advise or consult with a glv€n client depands on the particular
parties involved and subject matter. Without more information, we are unable to provide
specific advice and refer you generally to the provisions discussed in detail above.
6. The clientfiled an appeal of the cleanup and abatement order which you issued and
that order is in abeyance. You askwhether, assuming the appeal process proceeds to
active status, you could serve as an expert witness on behalf of the client?
The permanent ban covers any proceeding in which you personally participated
while with the water control board. (Brown Advice Letter, No. 4-91-033.) Given that the
order in question and the subject of the appeal is one which you ordered, the permanent
ban generally forbids you from rendering assistance to the clie,nt as it seeks to overturn
that order.
However, the permanent ban restricts only compensated activity, it does not
prevent you from representing or assisting anyperson on a volunteerbasis. Further, it
does not prevent you from representing the state or a state agency. ($$ 87401 and 57402-)
Section 87403 also contains some additional narrow exemptions from the
permanent revolving door prohibitions. These generally allow you to provide
uncompensated expert witness testimony or, in specific circumstances, and with the
permission of the Commission or a court, to participate more actively in a proceeding that
was pending during your tenure with the Commission. We encourage you to write for
further advice when the particulars of the potential participation in the appellate process
becomes more concrete.

CONCLUSION: 1. Yes. With certain rules limiting your contact with your former anployer, you
may advise the client regarding these issues.
2. Since the issuance of the cease and desist orders and notices of violation have
been finalized and issued already, the proceeding in which you had participated is now
complete. Therefore, you may participate in any manner in any issue involving
compliance with the orders, unless prohibited by the one-year ban. So long as advising
your client bears solely to compliance with already issued orders and does not pertain to
the issuance, amendment, awarding or revocation of a permit, or any other prohibited
activities, such representation is not prohibited by the one-year ban.
3. The permanent ban applies only to state administrative agencies. It does not
apply to local agencies. The one-year ban applies only to state administrative agencies.
The one-year ban does not apply to local agencies. However, any communication,
including meetings, with the regional board's staffthat is intended to influence any
administrative, legislative or other specified action by the regional board is prohibited by
the one-year ban. Thereforg you are prohibited, for a period of one year, from
participating at a local agency meeting in which regional board staffis prese,nt if your
participation is for the purpose of influencing any administrative, legislative or other
specified action by the regional board.
4. The permanent ban applies to judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceedings in
any state administrative agency in which you participated while anployed with the
regional board. So long as the govemmental bodies with which you wish to work do not
consist of the regional water control board with which you were ernployed, such activities
are not precluded. The one-year ban applies to the regional board, or any state agency
under that agency's control. As a general rule, you are not restricted by the one-year ban
from lobbying the Legislature or Governor regarding legislation. ln addition, you me not
prohibited from influencing adminiskative actions of agancies that are not under the
control of the regional board.
5. The revolving door provisions apply only to former public officials as
described in the various provisions. Thus, standing alone and without your advice or
participation, nothingprohibits your client from, on its own, pursuing anyparticular
activity. Whether you may advise or consult with a grven client depends on the particular
parties involved and subject matter. Without more information, we are unable to provide
specific advice and refer you generally to the provisions discussed in detail below.
6. See discussion below

Dickerson McGuire Environmental Consultants, Inc. 16870 W. Bernardo Center Drive, Suite 440 San Diego, CA 92127 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. I-04-156 Dear Mr. Dickerson: This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the post-governmental employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because we lack specific information identifying your potential client and the particulars of potential communications, we offer informal advice. Informal assistance does not confer the immunity provided by a Commission opinion or formal written advice. (Reg. 18329, subd. (c)(3), copy enclosed.) QUESTIONS 1.
